commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=71ebfa4a55b4dae780ea944df3d868ce40ab4768)
commands.review_summary:
Adds exec/bash `openclaw cron add` detection in the embedded tool-end handler and four regression tests that update `successfulCronAdds` from command output.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +129. Total +145 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main only counts structured cron tool adds while the reported flow uses exec/bash `openclaw cron add`, and the reply guard appends the note when `successfulCronAdds` remains zero. I did not run a live cron backend in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human PR review remains because the contributor needs real behavior proof and the branch still needs maintainer/author changes for Codex parity and exec success criteria.

target.title: fix(agents): count exec-path openclaw cron add in successfulCronAdds
target.body:
### What

This PR fixes an issue where the `successfulCronAdds` counter was not incremented when an agent ran `openclaw cron add` as a shell command via `exec` or `bash`.

### Why

The existing logic only checked for the structured `cron` tool. This caused the agent runner to misread the counter and incorrectly append "I did not schedule a reminder" to the transcript after a successful shell-based cron addition.

### How

An `else if` block is added to `pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` to inspect `exec`/`bash` results. It uses an anchored regex (`/^\s*openclaw\s+cron\s+add\b/i`) to identify the command and requires a success receipt (`created|scheduled`) in the output before incrementing the counter.

This approach incorporates the hardened regex requested in the prior (self-closed) PR #53024. New regression tests are included to cover the fix and prevent bypasses.

Closes #52972

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Agent runner incorrectly appends "I did not schedule a reminder" after the agent runs `openclaw cron add` via exec/bash shell tool (structured `cron` tool path was unaffected).

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw development clone, Node 24, Linux (WSL2). Exec/bash tool path exercised via the handler directly.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**
1. Agent issued `openclaw cron add "daily standup" "09:00"` via exec tool.
2. Exec result contained `Reminder created` in the aggregated output.
3. Inspected `ctx.state.successfulCronAdds` after handler returned.
4. Checked whether the "I did not schedule a reminder" note was appended to the transcript.

**Evidence after fix:**

Before fix — `successfulCronAdds` stays at 0 after a successful exec-path `openclaw cron add`, triggering the false "did not schedule" note.

After fix — handler reads the exec result, matches `/^\s*openclaw\s+cron\s+add\b/i` on the command, finds `created` in the aggregated output, and increments the counter. Bypass attempt (`echo "openclaw cron add ..."`) correctly stays at 0 because the anchored regex rejects substring matches.

Console output (branch-local run):
```
✓ increments successfulCronAdds when exec runs openclaw cron add and output confirms creation
✓ does not increment successfulCronAdds for a bypass attempt (substring match without anchor)
✓ does not increment successfulCronAdds when openclaw cron add output lacks creation receipt
✓ does not increment successfulCronAdds for a cron add command without the openclaw prefix
  24 passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** Counter increments on valid `openclaw cron add` via exec; false "I did not schedule a reminder" note is suppressed. Counter stays 0 on bypass attempts, failed runs, and unrelated commands.

**What was not tested:** Live OpenClaw server with real cron backend and actual scheduled jobs; agents using non-standard shell wrappers around `openclaw cron add`; exec timeout paths.
target.author: martingarramon
